expectations.text: Recommends running `winml analyze` against the exported ONNX as the first diagnostic step.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'Run analyze against your exported ONNX (not the HF id) and target QNN' with concrete command.

expectations.text: Explains analyze's linter will name the offending op pattern per EP.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'it lints every operator and pattern per EP and will name the offending op or pattern explicitly'.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend hand-editing the ONNX graph.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'do not hand-edit the ONNX graph'.

expectations.text: Suggests trying different optim/quant config OR escalating to 'not a fit'.
Encourages reading actual error message before guessing.
expectations.evidence: Asks user to share analyze JSON / 'just the offending pattern name'; lists Swin patterns 'If the error mentions a specific op'.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
